title: hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. patulum
intro: Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. patulum, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with reported medicinal properties including the ability to reduce swelling and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. patulum include its potential to support immune function and promote wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. patulum is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the plant's leaves and roots being used for medicinal purposes.
